Pastry Cook
Marble & Rye Bakery | Asheville, NC | 2020-2026

- Prepared laminated doughs for croissants, kouign-amann, morning buns, and
  seasonal Danish pastries using traditional folding and proofing techniques.
- Mixed, shaped, scored, and baked sourdough loaves, baguettes, focaccia, and
  rye breads for daily retail service.
- Maintained sourdough starters and preferments, adjusting hydration and
  fermentation timing based on kitchen temperature and flour behavior.
- Produced custards, pastry creams, ganache, fruit fillings, glazes, streusel,
  and buttercreams for cakes, tarts, and plated desserts.
- Decorated celebration cakes with piped borders, chocolate curls, fresh fruit,
  edible flowers, and custom lettering.
- Operated deck ovens, convection ovens, sheeters, mixers, proofers, scales, and
  refrigeration equipment during early morning production shifts.
- Followed sanitation procedures for food-contact surfaces, storage containers,
  cooling racks, and smallwares throughout service.
- Rotated perishable ingredients according to freshness dates and maintained
  organized dry storage, walk-in cooler, and freezer areas.
- Weighed ingredients precisely and followed batch formulas to keep product
  size, flavor, and texture consistent.
- Prepared seasonal menu items such as pumpkin spice brioche, lemon meringue
  tartlets, strawberry shortcake, and chocolate peppermint cookies.
- Packaged breads, pastries, cookies, and cakes for display cases, wholesale
  orders, farmers market stalls, and catering pickups.
- Coordinated daily bake lists with the head baker to ensure popular items were
  ready before the morning rush.
- Trained new kitchen staff on safe knife handling, mixer operation, dough
  shaping, oven loading, and cleaning routines.
- Checked finished products for color, crumb, rise, lamination, moisture, and
  presentation before sending them to the sales counter.
- Reduced ingredient waste by reusing trim for bread pudding, croutons, tart
  shells, and staff meal components.
- Assisted with inventory counts for flour, butter, sugar, eggs, chocolate,
  fruit puree, yeast, parchment, boxes, and labels.
- Fulfilled special orders for birthdays, weddings, office breakfasts, holiday
  trays, and local restaurant accounts.
- Worked standing shifts in a hot kitchen while lifting flour bags, sheet pans,
  mixing bowls, and ingredient bins.
- Communicated with front-of-house staff about allergens, sell-out timing,
  product descriptions, and customer pickup details.
- Closed the pastry station by wrapping mise en place, labeling containers,
  sweeping floors, washing tools, and setting up the next day's production.
